plan.title: Document provider-neutral sub-agent routing in the swarm skill

narratives.Description: This story updates the swarm operator guidance so heterogeneous dispatch is described as a provider-neutral capability rather than a Grok Build-only path. It is independently buildable because it owns the swarm skill text and does not need to modify dispatch templates or tests directly.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the source file skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md to separate dispatch provider, worker role, and model or agent selection in the heterogeneous dispatch guidance.
2. Verify the change with focused content tests in tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py by ensuring the skill text exposes provider-neutral backend evidence and safe role-boundary assertions.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want the swarm skill to describe sub-agent backend choice independently of Grok Build, so that I can route leaf coding work to Composer or another provider without losing Deft guardrails.

items.title: Given an operator reads the swarm skill, when they reach heterogeneous dispatch guidance, then it explicitly separates dispatch provider, worker role, and model or agent selection.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an operator reads the swarm skill, when they reach heterogeneous dispatch guidance, then it explicitly separates dispatch provider, worker role, and model or agent selection.

items.title: Given the guidance names supported backend examples, then it includes Composer-class coding agents, Grok Build spawn_subagent workers, Cursor or cloud agents, and future adapters without treating any one backend as mandatory.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the guidance names supported backend examples, then it includes Composer-class coding agents, Grok Build spawn_subagent workers, Cursor or cloud agents, and future adapters without treating any one backend as mandatory.

items.title: Given a user considers cheaper leaf agents, then the skill states that orchestration, review-cycle decisions, conflict-resolution rebase, merge, and release gates remain on strong or review-capable agents.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a user considers cheaper leaf agents, then the skill states that orchestration, review-cycle decisions, conflict-resolution rebase, merge, and release gates remain on strong or review-capable agents.

expected_outputs: swarm skill guidance carries provider-neutral backend terminology and safety boundaries

references.title: Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch (strong-model orchestrator + cheap high-context leaf code-runners)
Issue #1531: swarm: opt-in tiered / heterogeneous dispatch
